one_page_architecture_summary_v0_1.title: Reliability Architecture for AI Under Uncertainty — Internal Review Stack v0.1
one_page_architecture_summary_v0_1.status: internal reliability-review architecture, not a validated product
one_page_architecture_summary_v0_1.plain_english_summary: This is an internal method for stress-testing AI behavior when facts are incomplete, authority is ambiguous, or physical and digital worlds drift apart. Instead of relying on generic disclaimers, the stack challenges a system with edge-case prompts, reviews its responses for honesty about evidence, failure boundaries, and operational limits, and then feeds those insights back into design. It does not certify a system; it shows where the system's reasoning breaks.
one_page_architecture_summary_v0_1.problem_addressed: Advanced AI systems can sound confident even when they lack supporting facts, misinterpret context, or fail to notice that a digital record no longer reflects physical reality. Existing testing often focuses on accuracy against a known answer; this stack focuses on epistemic behavior: how a system labels uncertainty, admits proxy use, flags missing authority, handles mismatches, and identifies recovery paths.
one_page_architecture_summary_v0_1.method: The stack maintains a structured internal adversarial suite with 25 prompts targeting failure families such as synthetic data drift, scope overextension, analog-digital fracture, degraded infrastructure triage, and cross-cultural distress misclassification. Each case includes a visible failure_column and structured review dimensions covering provenance, observable anchors, proxy honesty, claim boundaries, workflow traceability, human-review triggers, operational impact, and recovery paths. Results are not public scores; they are internal maps of where a scaffold fails, used to tighten design before deployment decisions.
what_it_can_claim: The stack can identify specific, reproducible failure modes in an AI system's handling of incomplete, ambiguous, or cross-context information.
It provides a structured way to compare a scaffold's claimed reliability behavior against its actual behavior in synthetic edge-case scenarios.
It generates internal evidence that can guide design improvements, training-data requirements, review thresholds, and escalation policies.
what_it_cannot_claim: It is not a benchmark, certification, audit replacement, compliance product, security tool, or safety guarantee.
It does not guarantee safety, security, regulatory compliance, or deployment readiness.
It does not replace human audit, legal review, professional review, or real-world monitoring.
It does not measure model accuracy on standard tasks; it reviews reliability-oriented behavior under uncertainty.
It is not yet a finished commercial product; it is an internal draft review architecture and artifact stack.
one_page_architecture_summary_v0_1.buyer_safe_description: Internal reliability-review stack for enterprise AI systems. Designed to surface hidden failure modes when a system must operate with incomplete or conflicting information, dynamic authority structures, or mismatches between digital records and physical reality. Provides a structured method for testing how honestly a system labels evidence, where it sets operational boundaries, and whether it escalates or recovers appropriately.
one_page_architecture_summary_v0_1.failure_column: This architecture stops being useful if: (a) the target AI system has no scaffold-like structure to test; (b) the prompts require ground-truth answers the evaluator cannot verify; (c) the results are presented publicly as a performance score rather than an internal debugging map; or (d) the adversarial suite becomes static and is no longer updated to reflect emerging failure modes.
evidence_needed_for_stronger_claims: Repeated, independent application of the suite across multiple systems with consistent, interpretable failure patterns.
Correlation between suite results and observed reliability outcomes in real workflows.
Review of the failure-family taxonomy and structured review dimensions by reliability engineers, human-factors experts, and domain specialists.
Demonstration that the suite can be maintained over time without drifting from its intended epistemic targets.
Controlled studies showing that scaffold improvements driven by suite findings lead to measurable reduction in high-severity incidents.

calibration_dataset_skeleton_v0_1.title: Unknown-Domain AI Reliability Behavior Dataset Skeleton v0.1
calibration_dataset_skeleton_v0_1.status: internal draft dataset skeleton, not a validated benchmark
calibration_dataset_skeleton_v0_1.purpose: Define a structured data format for testing how AI systems handle epistemic uncertainty, authority gaps, physical-digital drift, and cross-context distress signals in synthetic, domain-agnostic scenarios. Each record is an adversarial prompt paired with target failure modes, structured checks, and a required visible failure_column. The dataset is designed for internal use: it can calibrate scaffold behavior, compare versions, or feed design decisions, but it must not be presented as a public benchmark, leaderboard, certification, or safety guarantee.

record_schema.case_id: Unique identifier such as synth_01 or fracture_03
record_schema.category: Failure-family label from a controlled vocabulary
record_schema.synthetic_context: Brief scenario background to ground the prompt
record_schema.adversarial_prompt: The exact prompt given to the AI system under test
record_schema.target_failure_mode: Concise description of the specific epistemic or operational failure being tested
record_schema.expected_weak_pattern: Typical weak response pattern used to guide human review, not a machine target answer
strong_response_checks: Atomic, checkable assertions that should be true of a strong response
record_schema.failure_column: The visible falsifiable condition that indicates the scaffold has failed in this scenario
record_schema.human_review_required: Boolean or threshold-based flag indicating whether this case requires human evaluation
record_schema.notes: Internal-use notes, case-design provenance, or version tracking

acceptance_rules: All case_id values must be unique.
category must be one of the controlled categories.
adversarial_prompt must be a non-empty string.
target_failure_mode must be a non-empty string.
failure_column must contain at least one falsifiable condition.
strong_response_checks must be a non-empty list of atomic checks.
scoring_dimensions must include failure_column_quality while preserving the visible case-level failure_column.
human_review_required must be boolean or threshold-based.
The dataset must not contain references to named AI models or vendors.
The dataset must not be distributed publicly as a benchmark, leaderboard, certification, compliance product, or safety guarantee.
calibration_dataset_skeleton_v0_1.failure_column: This dataset skeleton stops being useful if: (a) the controlled categories do not cover the main failure families observed in the target scaffold; (b) strong_response_checks become too generic to distinguish weak and strong responses; (c) the dataset is used as a public scoreboard; (d) synthetic contexts become stale; or (e) human reviewers consistently override machine-checkable rules, indicating the checks need recalibration.
